items.title: US-Iran deal: latest developments
items.summary: Months of conflict, initiated by US and Israeli strikes on Iran in February, wreaked chaos across the region and rattled the global economy until a peace deal was announced last week. Here are the latest developments ahead of the signing: TRUMP THREATENS IRAN US President Donald Trump on Wednesday warned Iran he was ready to resume military action if Teheran did not abide by its obligations. Although the text has not been released, US and Iranian officials have said that the agreement covers the fighting in Lebanon.

items.title: Anwar says he enjoys Russian songs, cites love of literature
items.summary: KAZAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has revealed a fondness for Russian music and literature, saying cultural exchanges are just as important as economic cooperation in strengthening ties between nations. Asked whether he had a favourite Russian song, Anwar instead spoke about his appreciation of Russian literature and poetry. Anwar said cultural exchanges were vital in fostering understanding between people and countries.

items.title: Hamzah, Pas leaders meet in KL amid deepening rift with Bersatu
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Several Pas leaders were seen arriving at the party's headquarters in Jalan Raja Laut today for an opposition members of parliament pre-council meeting. The meeting comes as Perikatan Nasional faces a political crossroads following a deepening rift between Pas and Bersatu. The meeting follows a Pas Central Committee meeting earlier in the afternoon.

items.title: PM Anwar discusses Asean-Russia ties, culture and global issues with international media in Kazan
items.summary: KAZAN, June 17 — From Asean-Russia cooperation and energy security to Russian literature and the humanitarian crisis in Gaza, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim fielded a wide range of questions from international media on the sidelines of the Asean-Russia Business Forum here. Anwar said closer collaboration could be pursued in areas including energy security, cybersecurity, agriculture, digital technology, scientific research and higher education. The Prime Minister also described his recent meeting with Russian President Vladimir Putin as productive, particularly in discussions involving economic cooperation and energy security.

items.title: Asean, Russia must expand cooperation in food security, energy – Anwar
items.summary: KAZAN: Asean and Russia should broaden multilateral cooperation in key sectors including food security, energy, advanced manufacturing, digital technologies and education, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said these areas presented significant opportunities for collaboration as Asean member states and Russia seek to diversify markets, strengthen supply chain resilience and explore new sources of growth. Despite global uncertainty, he added that Asean had shown resilience by continuing to attract investment, deepen integration and strengthen its position within global value chains.

items.title: Kiandee: Hamzah has majority support, Speaker has final say on opposition leader
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Dewan Rakyat speaker has the final say on who leads the opposition bloc and commands their support, said Beluran member of parliament Datuk Seri Dr Ronald Kiandee. In a statement today, Kiandee said that Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin remains secure as the opposition leader as he commands a clear majority backing from Pas en-bloc and the majority of Bersatu, and cannot be undermined by any internal party resolution. "The statement provides legitimacy to Hamzah as the opposition leader, backed by clear and collective majority support from the opposition bloc of MPs," Kiandee said, adding that the en-bloc support from Pas lawmakers strengthens the parliamentary check-and-balance structure.

items.title: Annuar urges Bersatu to stay calm amid PN tensions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bersatu has been urged to remain calm and refrain from making premature statements on the latest developments within Perikatan Nasional (PN) amid ongoing tensions in the coalition. PN information chief Tan Sri Annuar Musa said no PN Supreme Council meeting had been held today, despite speculation surrounding the coalition's next move. "Is there a PN Supreme Council meeting today?

items.title: Only Trump can decide on call with Taiwan leader, Taipei says
items.summary: Trump said following a summit with China's Xi Jinping in Beijing last month that he would speak with Taiwan's President Lai Ching-te. Speaking to the Taiwan Foreign Correspondents Club in Taipei, Taiwan's Foreign Minister Lin Chia-lung said only Trump knows when a call may take place. Taiwan rejects Beijing's sovereignty claims.

items.title: Anwar: Finalisation of Asean-Russia trade roadmap a good start
items.summary: KAZAN: Finalisation of the Asean-Russia Strategic Programme on Trade and Investment Cooperation 2026-2035 is a shot in the arm for the next leap in economic ties, but an enabling environment is key to turning them into reality, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. The programme, set to be adopted later this year, provides the roadmap for the next phase of economic relations between the 11-member bloc and Russia, focusing on trade and investment, emerging technologies, and climate resilience. "Economic resilience should complement international cooperation, not replace it," he said.

items.title: Teo alleges manipulated posters aimed at undermining PH support
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Johor DAP chairman Teo Nie Ching today alleged that certain parties manipulated posters of potential candidates to undermine support for Pakatan Harapan (PH) in the Johor election. Teo said the altered posters portrayed the potential candidates as Muslim women wearing their headscarves improperly. The Wanita DAP chief said the party had consistently championed the rights of all Malaysians regardless of race, background, region, religion or gender.

items.title: Chen Zhi scam syndicate leader extradited to China
items.summary: BEIJING: An alleged leading member of a prominent crime syndicate that operated scam centres in Cambodia has been extradited back to China, Beijing said on Wednesday. China's Ministry of Public Security (MPS) published a video on Wednesday showing a man it identified as Liu Ren in handcuffs as security forces lifted a black bag off his head, after he had been escorted off a China Southern plane. "Recently, with the strong support of relevant Cambodian authorities, a task force dispatched by the Ministry of Public Security successfully extradited Liu Ren – a key member of the Chen Zhi criminal syndicate – back to China from Phnom Penh," the ministry said in a statement.

items.title: Diesel price in peninsular Malaysia now RM4.37 a litre, cheaper by 30 sen; RON95 and RON97 prices maintained
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 17 — The retail price of diesel in Peninsular Malaysia has been reduced by 30 sen to RM4.37 per litre for the week of June 18–24, following a decline in international market prices, the Finance Ministry said in a statement today. The ministry said the adjustment was made under the Automatic Pricing Mechanism (APM) and reflected average movement of international market prices in the previous week, noting that easing tensions in West Asia had helped moderate global crude oil prices compared to recent peaks. For the June 18–24 period, retail prices are as follows: RON97: RM4.35 per litre (unchanged) RON95: RM3.72 per litre (unchanged) Diesel (Peninsular Malaysia): RM4.37 per litre (down 30 sen) Subsidised fuel prices remain unchanged, including RM1.99 per litre for RON95 under BUDI95, RM2.15 per litre for diesel in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan, and RM2.05–RM2.15 per litre under the SKPS and SKDS schemes.

items.title: Why a strong El Nino puts cocoa, coffee and sugar crops at risk
items.summary: What is El Nino and why are commodities grown in tropical regions, known as soft commodities, especially exposed? EL NINO El Nino is a periodic warming of sea surface temperatures in the eastern Pacific caused by weakening trade winds. Soft commodities have consistently seen strong price gains during past El Nino episodes.

items.title: PM Anwar: Asean-Russia should expand cooperation more broadly, especially in energy and food security
items.summary: KAZAN, June 17 — Asean and Russia need to expand multilateral cooperation in areas such as food security, energy, advanced manufacturing, digital technologies, and education, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He noted that these sectors offer significant opportunities for collaboration as Asean member countries and Russia seek to diversify markets, strengthen supply chain resilience and explore new avenues for growth. “Despite the uncertain global environment, Asean has demonstrated remarkable resilience.

items.title: 'I'm the boss,' Trump tells G7 leaders
items.summary: EVIAN, France: The G7 summit of world powers in France is being chaired by President Emmanuel Macron as host but on Wednesday his guest US President Donald Trump left no doubt over who he believed was in charge. Amid laughter, Macron appeared to take the comment with good humour. the French president asked.

items.title: Trump's Iran accord offers exit from war and fresh political risks
items.summary: THE Trump administration and Iran's leadership agreed on Sunday to terms to end their war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a development that has delivered significant relief to global markets. "Trump has surrendered to Iran," conservative influencer Erick Erickson wrote on X. US Senator Lindsey Graham, a Trump ally who advocated for the war, said in a Sunday X post he was "somewhat concerned" that Iran's description of the preliminary agreement was different from the White House's description.

items.title: Don't 'win the battle but lose the war', Pas MP warns Bersatu ahead of state polls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A Pas member of parliament has warned Bersatu that contesting against the party in the Johor and Negri Sembilan state elections would only hand victory to Pakatan Harapan. Bachok MP Mohd Syahir Che Sulaiman said that the use of the Perikatan Nasional (PN) logo and candidate endorsement letters should be decided by the coalition's supreme council and endorsed by its chairman, Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar. He was responding to Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin's statement that the party had decided to remain in PN and would contest the Johor and Negri Sembilan elections using the coalition's logo.

items.title: Puad to sit out Johor election, cites age
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former Johor assembly Speaker Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi has confirmed he will not contest the Rengit seat in the Johor election on July 11, citing age as the primary reason behind his decision. In a Facebook post, Puad said he had made the decision a year ago and informed Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi and Johor Umno liaison committee chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi of his intention two weeks ago. Puad said Onn Hafiz, who is also the caretaker menteri besar, should be given the opportunity to lead a younger generation of candidates and the next Johor government.

items.title: Asean-Russia needs to expand cooperation across the board, mainly in food security, energy – Anwar
items.summary: KAZAN: Asean and Russia need to expand multilateral cooperation in areas such as food security, energy, advanced manufacturing, digital technologies, and education, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He noted that these sectors offer significant opportunities for collaboration as Asean member countries and Russia seek to diversify markets, strengthen supply chain resilience and explore new avenues for growth. "Despite the uncertain global environment, Asean has demonstrated remarkable resilience.

items.title: Hawana: Police boost security and traffic control, main roads stay open
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Penang police have assured that the level of security and traffic in conjunction with the National Journalists' Day (Hawana) 2026 celebration at PICCA Convention Centre @ Butterworth Arena, near here, will be smooth without affecting the daily movements of local residents. Penang police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said various proactive measures have been put in place to ensure the smooth running of the prestigious event, including traffic management and comprehensive security control as implemented during the Malaysia Day 2025 celebration at the same location last year. "The strength of personnel assigned to security and traffic control is also the same as that implemented during the previous Malaysia Day celebration," he said when contacted by Bernama today.

items.title: Opening Hormuz will be 'massive step forward': NATO chief
items.summary: BRUSSELS: NATO chief Mark Rutte on Wednesday hailed the US-Iran deal to end the Middle East war, saying the planned reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, would be a "massive step forward." "The restoration of free passage through the Strait of Hormuz will be a massive step forward, and I know that many allies, through the initiative led by France and the United Kingdom, are ready to support," Rutte told a press conference in Brussels. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Jakarta's cinema city ambition gets boost from Neflix
items.summary: JAKARTA: Jakarta's ambition to become a Cinema City has gained momentum after Netflix expressed interest in expanding cooperation on film production and talent development in the Indonesian capital. Netflix Asia Pacific Public Policy Director Ruben Hattari said strong local content and sustained audience demand have been major drivers of the company's growth across the Asia-Pacific region, adding that the platform hopes to expand cooperation with Jakarta in film production and talent development. The Jakarta administration said at least three Netflix film and series projects are planned for production in the city, while Jakarta and Netflix are also preparing a human resource development programme to support the industry's growth.

items.title: Israeli settlers torched West Bank mosque: Palestinian official
items.summary: JILJILIYA: Israeli settlers set fire to a mosque in a West Bank village on Wednesday, the local mayor said, while AFP journalists at the site saw signs of arson and vandalism. Osama Abdullah, head of the village council in Jiljiliya, north of Ramallah, told AFP that "settlers set fire to the ablution room, caused damage to the village's main mosque, and scrawled hostile slogans on the outer walls." Israel has occupied the West Bank since 1967.

items.title: Prolonged downpour led to mass fish deaths at Danau Kota lake, says DBKL
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The recent mass death of fish at Tasik Taman Danau Kota here is believed to have been triggered by continuous heavy rainfall. "Such conditions can affect aquatic life and threaten the survival of fish," it said. DBKL said the cleanup works to remove dead fish had been carried out, while rubbish traps were installed at the lake's inlet channels.

items.title: US-Iran MoU a fragile start, analysts say
items.summary: Pacific Research Centre of Malaysia principal adviser Dr Oh Ei Sun said the arrangement was merely a temporary ceasefire. On Lebanon specifically, Oh said the durability of any peace framework would depend on Iran's ability to rein in Hezbollah and other groups, despite Teheran's limited control over some of them. He highlighted Iran's claim that US$12 billion would be released as part of the arrangement.

items.title: BMW shares hit 2020 lows after China weakness and Iran war prompt profit warning
items.summary: BERLIN: Shares in premium German automaker BMW fell around 7.0 per cent after it issued a profit warning late on Tuesday that some analysts said could herald a broader strategic rethink, including capacity cuts in Europe. Wednesday's price fall took BMW shares to their lowest level since November 2020 and weighed on shares across the European auto sector, including German rivals Volkswagen and Mercedes-Benz. In addition to lowering its operating auto margin to 1.0 per cent to 3.0 per cent, from 4.0 per cent to 6.0 per cent previously, BMW said it would intensify cost-cutting, with a negative one-off in the second half of 2026.

items.title: UK's Hays sells six European units, considers further exits as hiring downturn persists
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: British recruiter Hays said on Wednesday that it has sold its businesses in six European countries and is considering more exits, as a prolonged hiring slowdown in the region pushes the group to sharpen its focus on core operations. * Hays has completed the sale of its Czech Republic, Denmark, Hungary, Luxembourg, Romania, and Sweden businesses to private equity firm Meraki Capital for £4 million (US$5.4 million). * Hays said it now plans to focus on its remaining 16 core countries.

items.title: Maintenance worker drowns after being pulled into water tank
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 24-year-old man drowned after he was believed to have been pulled into a water tank while carrying out maintenance work at a water tower in Bandar Saujana Perdana near here yesterday. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department operations centre chief Hamdan Hamzah said the victim was found unconscious on top of the tower reservoir tank and was pronounced dead at the scene. "Based on preliminary information, the victim was believed to have been pulled into the water tank and drowned while carrying out maintenance work at the tower reservoir tank," Hamdan said in a statement today.

items.title: ISA detention became turning point in life, says Kedah Maal Hijrah recipient
items.summary: ALOR SETAR: A two-week detention under the Internal Security Act (ISA) in his youth proved to be a life-changing experience for Kedah's Tokoh Maal Hijrah, Dr Shukri Abdullah. The 76-year-old said the incident, which occurred in 1974 following his involvement in the Baling Demonstrations while serving as a student leader at Universiti Sains Malaysia (USM), gave him a renewed appreciation for education and the importance of planning for the future. The motivational speaker said he devoted himself entirely to his studies after his release and eventually graduated as USM's overall best student.

items.title: PublicInvest cuts airlines to Neutral as rising costs threaten margins
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Airlines are expected to remain profitable, but margins could come under pressure from higher fuel and operating costs, coupled with limited scope for further efficiency gains, according to Public Investment Bank Bhd (PublicInvest). According to PublicInvest, jet fuel markets remain a key pressure point. PublicInvest also expects slower economic growth ahead, forecasting global gross domestic product growth to ease to 2.5 per cent in 2026 from around three per cent.

items.title: Louvre museum 'running out of steam', says new director
items.summary: PARIS: The Louvre museum is struggling to find funding to upgrade its ageing facilities, its new director said Wednesday, following a litany of problems that included a brazen US$100-million jewellery heist. Leribault, an art historian and museum director specialising in 18th-century art, took the helm in February, after his predecessor stepped down following an embarrassing daylight theft of imperial jewels in October that laid bare severe security failings. Home to iconic pieces of art including Leonardo da Vinci's "Mona Lisa", the Louvre is the world's most visited museum, receiving around nine million visitors a year.

items.title: Puad Zarkashi ends six‑term run in Rengit, says age makes re‑election inappropriate
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 17 — Johor legislative assembly speaker Datuk Mohd Puad Zarkashi has confirmed that he would not defend his incumbent Rengit seat in the upcoming state election. “In fact, I conveyed my intention to Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi and the party’s Johor state chief Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi about two weeks ago,” he said in a posting on his official Facebook page today. Puad, who is also an Umno supreme council member, said Onn Hafiz, as the Johor Menteri Besar, should be given the opportunity to lead the younger generation and the state government.

items.title: Why are some people mosquito magnets? Clues are emerging
items.summary: EVER felt like mosquitoes bite you while ignoring everyone else? "It's not a misconception – mosquitoes are attracted to some people more than others," Frederic Simard of France's Institute of Research for Development told AFP. The idea that mosquitoes prefer particular blood types "has no scientific basis," Simard said.

items.title: ‘Not chicken feed’: 1MDB judge says Najib’s reaction to US$681m deposits not that of a reasonable man
items.summary: In his 809-page judgment released yesterday, 1MDB trial judge Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah said evidence such as bank records and the money trail showed that the US$681 million was actually misappropriated from 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB), also rejecting Najib’s claim that it was an Arab donation. The judge pointed out that Najib did not verify if the money he received was actually donations from Saudi Arabia. But the judge said Najib didn’t verify if the money was actually from King Abdullah.

items.title: HLIB: Deleum's 2H FY26 outlook stays positive on oilfield services strength, resilient orderbook
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Deleum Bhd's earnings outlook for the second half of financial year 2026 (2H FY26) remains positive, supported by solid oilfield services momentum and a strong orderbook, Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB) said. "The oilfield services (OIS) segment is expected to remain the key growth driver, supported by newly secured slickline contracts and recovering maintenance, chemicals and measurement (MCM) activities," it said. Revenue fell 39 per cent quarter-on-quarter (QoQ), mainly due to lower P&M contributions from reduced gas turbine, safety valve and flow regulator sales.

items.title: Perak Perhilitan relocates herd of 12 wild elephants from Kampung Bongor to forest reserve
items.summary: GERIK, June 17 — The Perak Department of Wildlife and National Parks (Perhilitan) has managed to relocate a herd of 12 wild elephants in the area of Kampung Bongor in a recent operation. Its director Mohd Taufik Abdul Rahman said that the elephants were moved in stages from the village since Monday with the help of two female elephants Indah and Suria and will be relocated to their natural habitat far from human settlements. “All adult and young elephants found were estimated to be about one and a half years to 35 years old,” he said when contacted by Bernama, adding that the department received complaints from villagers about the presence of elephants in their area recently.

items.title: Maintenance worker, 24, drowns during water tower tank repair in Bandar Saujana Perdana
items.summary: Selangor Fire and Rescue Department operations centre chief Hamdan Hamzah said the 24-year-old victim was found unconscious on top of the tower reservoir tank before being pronounced dead at the scene. “The fire engine arrived at the scene at 12.17pm and found the victim unconscious. Hamdan said firefighters subsequently retrieved and brought the victim down from the tank.

items.title: Frameworks alone not enough, Anwar urges businesses and innovators to drive Asean‑Russia cooperation
items.summary: KAZAN, June 17 — Finalisation of the Asean-Russia Strategic Programme on Trade and Investment Cooperation 2026-2035 is a shot in the arm for the next leap in economic ties, but an enabling environment is key to turning them into reality, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. The programme, set to be adopted later this year, provides the roadmap for the next phase of economic relations between the 11-member bloc and Russia, focusing on trade and investment, emerging technologies, and climate resilience. “Economic resilience should complement international cooperation, not replace it,” he said.

items.title: Nanta confirms he will remain politically active, laughs off speculation on next move
items.summary: KUCHING, June 17 — Gabungan Parti Sarawak (GPS) secretary-general Datuk Seri Alexander Nanta Linggi remained tight-lipped on Wednesday over whether he would defend his parliamentary seat or transition to state politics. Pressed about his political future amid ongoing speculation over candidate negotiations for the coming Sarawak election, Nanta said the matter had already received extensive media coverage. Nanta, who is also Parti Pesaka Bumiputera Bersatu (PBB) secretary-general and Kapit MP, indicated that there were plans for his next political move but said he was not ready to disclose further details.

items.title: Kelantan police say petrol smuggling at Malaysia‑Thailand border remains low, controlled despite West Asia tensions
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 17 — Petrol smuggling activities along the Malaysia-Thailand border, particularly in Kelantan, remain at a low and controlled level despite concerns over the impact of ongoing tensions in West Asia on regional fuel supply. According to him, the situation remains manageable due to proactive and coordinated enforcement measures conducted through large-scale integrated operations at border entry points and petrol stations within identified risk zones. Mohd Yusoff said daily control operations and patrols conducted by the General Operations Force (GOF), together with police personnel from nearby stations, would continue to be intensified at identified petrol stations to prevent any leakage of subsidised fuel.

items.title: Hobart Camp grenade blast kills soldier’s daughter Siti Khadijah, who dreamed of following father’s legacy
items.summary: KLUANG, June 17 — A retired soldier’s dream of seeing his youngest daughter continuing the family’s military legacy ended in tragedy when Private Siti Khadijah Sungip, 24, was killed during a grenade exercise at Hobart Camp in Gurun, Kedah, yesterday. The victim’s elder sister, Siti Zaiha, 39, said Siti Khadijah sent a photograph via WhatsApp at midnight on June 15, which is now the family’s last keepsake. “Siti Khadijah said she had more training the next day and that the grenade exercise would start around 8am.

items.title: Minister warns false claims of 60,000 backdoor entries risk eroding trust in higher education
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 17 — The allegation that between 30,000 and 60,000 students gained admission to public universities (UA) through the ‘backdoor’ is a serious accusation that could undermine public confidence in the integrity of the country’s higher education system. Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir said the baseless claim not only tarnishes the reputation of public universities but also creates the perception that opportunities meant for qualified students had been taken away for financial gain. He said such a narrative could erode public trust, particularly among parents and students currently undergoing the admission process to higher education institutions (IPTs).

items.title: Mass fish kill in Danau Kota: DBKL explains why heavy rain may be to blame
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 17 — City Hall (DBKL) has responded to reports of a mass fish kill at Taman Danau Kota Lake yesterday, confirming that comprehensive clean-up operations were completed on the same day. “DBKL, alongside the Federal Territory’s Department of Environment, also conducted water sampling on June 16 for quality analysis to pinpoint the exact factors contributing to the incident,” DBKL said. DBKL noted that Taman Danau Kota Lake serves a dual purpose: it is a recreational park that also functions as a flood retention pond.

items.title: Protect e‑hailing drivers: Lee Lam Thye calls for dashcams, panic buttons and tougher checks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 17 — Protecting e-hailing drivers must be made a national priority amid increasing reports of assault, intimidation, robbery and violence by unruly passengers, said Alliance for a Safe Community chairman Tan Sri Lee Lam Thye. He said the safety and well-being of e-hailing drivers must therefore be given greater attention by all stakeholders, including the government, e-hailing platform operators, law enforcement agencies and passengers themselves. “Law enforcement agencies must continue to act firmly against individuals who assault, threaten or harass e-hailing drivers.

items.title: Malaysian Army chief orders immediate halt to grenade drills after deadly accident in Kedah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 17 — The Malaysian Army has halted all grenade technique exercises with immediate effect following a deadly training incident at Hobart Camp, Kedah, which killed two soldiers yesterday. Army chief General Tan Sri Azhan Md Othman said the suspension is a necessary step to facilitate a comprehensive probe into the incident, NST reported. “We have suspended grenade-throwing training with immediate effect to allow investigations to be carried out,” he said.

items.title: Anwar hails TV AlHijrah’s role in Islamic broadcasting as station turns 16
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 17 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said that broadcasting grounded in values, knowledge and wisdom continues to hold a special place in society despite rapid changes and growing challenges in the media landscape. In a Facebook post marking the 16th anniversary of TV AlHijrah today, he expressed hope that the milestone would spur the station to further progress as a platform for public enlightenment. He said the anniversary should serve as a catalyst for TV AlHijrah to advance its role in promoting moderate Islamic understanding, nurturing human values and supporting the Malaysia MADANI vision.

items.title: No new leads as search for two missing fishermen in Kemaman enters Day 7
items.summary: CHUKAI, June 17 — The search and rescue operation (Carilamat) for two fishermen who are feared missing after their boat sank in Kemaman waters on June 10 continued today, with the search area covering 50.40 square nautical miles. Kemaman Zone Maritime director Abdul Halim Hamzah said the operation, which entered its seventh day, involved 19 members of the Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) and eight members of the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM). He said the assets involved an MMEA ship, KM Redang, with 19 members, and a PDRM boat, PLC 3.

items.title: World Cup 2026: RTM apologises after technical glitch disrupts France-Senegal live stream for Malaysian fans
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 17 – Radio Televisyen Malaysia (RTM) has issued a formal apology to football fans following a technical glitch on its RTMKlik platform that disrupted the live broadcast of the France-Senegal World Cup match earlier today. In a statement today, the national broadcaster attributed the failure to an “unforeseen technical issue.” RTM confirmed that its technical team and technology providers acted immediately to restore the service as quickly as possible. As the official broadcaster of the Fifa World Cup 2026, RTM is tasked with airing all 104 matches of the tournament, including 94 live broadcasts and 10 delayed telecasts.

items.title: IIUM rector Osman Bakar is Malaysia’s Tokoh Maal Hijrah 2026
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 17 — International Islamic University Malaysia (IIUM) Rector Prof Emeritus Datuk Dr Osman Bakar was named the National Tokoh Maal Hijrah at the national-level Maal Hijrah 1448H/2026 celebration at Putra Mosque here today. At the same ceremony, renowned Islamic scholar from Morocco, Dr Ahmad Al-Raysuni, was honoured as the International Tokoh Maal Hijrah. This year’s National Maal Hijrah celebration carried the theme “MADANI Dihayati, Ummah Diberkati”, reflecting efforts to strengthen values of compassion, unity and community well-being.

items.title: Works minister says rising construction material costs still manageable, contractors able to absorb increases
items.summary: KUCHING, June 17 — The increase in the cost of construction materials following the global energy crisis and disruptions to the world’s supply chain is still at a manageable level. Works Minister Datuk Seri Alexander Nanta Linggi said that for now, contractors are still able to absorb the cost increase and continue with the project without any delays. He said the study included short-term and long-term strategies to deal with any economic impact, especially those involving the supply chain and the cost of construction materials.

items.title: Energy sector in focus as Anwar says Tatarstan’s oil strength offers Malaysia downstream and petrochemical opportunities
items.summary: KAZAN, June 17 — Malaysia and the Republic of Tatarstan have vast potential to expand cooperation in various fields, including trade, investment, education, tourism, the halal industry, technology and talent development, said Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, said the energy sector was among the key areas of discussion, given that Tatarstan is one of the major oil-producing regions in Russia. Anwar said he and Minnikhanov also discussed the role of the Kazan Forum in strengthening business ties while creating new opportunities in innovation, the digital economy and strategic investments.

items.title: Villagers discover body of 48‑year‑old man in Kampung Togudon forest, Penampang police say struck by falling tree
items.summary: PENAMPANG, June 17 — A man was found dead inside a forest at Kampung Togudon here on Monday. Penampang district police chief Supt Sammy Newton said villagers discovered the 48-year-old victim lying on the ground at around 6am. “Police investigation revealed that the victim had entered the forest alone on June 10 to check and maintain his bat net.

items.title: Sabah’s Papar district to gain extra 10 million litres daily as water projects near completion, says Armizan
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 17 — Two ongoing water infrastructure construction projects in Papar will be operational this year to enable a long-term solution to the issue of clean water supply disruptions in the district is realised. Papar Member of Parliament Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said the commitment was given by the contractor in a special meeting with the Sabah State Water Department (JANS) and related agencies chaired by the Sabah State Secretary (SKN) recently. The Minister of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living said the Kampung Kabang water intake upgrading project was given a commitment to be completed by July 31, while the Kogopon 2 Water Treatment Plant (WTP) component is targeted to be operational in stages from August to September.

items.title: Op Drain: MACC nabs 13 including govt agency director over ‘contract cartel’ scandal
items.summary: IPOH, June 17 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has secured remand orders for 13 individuals, including a current and former director of a government agency, following allegations of a RM2.5 million bribery scheme involving public contracts. Three suspects, including two civil servants and a company director, will remain in custody until today. The remaining 10 suspects face a five-day remand expiring on June 20.

items.title: No more ‘green wave’?: PAS sounds the alarm over parties going after the 40pc youth vote
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 17 — PAS is concerned with the emergence of new political parties specifically designed to attract young voters ahead of the next general election, describing the trend as a significant strategic challenge. PAS vice-president Datuk Mohd Amar Nik Abdullah noted that these new entities appear to have meticulously mapped out their target demographics, focusing on youth who remain unaligned with any existing political party, NST reported. “What they say can easily influence the minds of young voters.

items.title: Diarrhoea, vomiting claims spark probe at Janda Baik river, Pahang Health Dept steps up checks
items.summary: KUANTAN, June 17 — The Pahang State Health Department (JKNP) is conducting an investigation following allegations that several visitors experienced symptoms of diarrhoea, vomiting and fever after bathing in a river at a recreational area near Bentong recently. JKNP in a statement announced that a risk assessment had been carried out and the results of the initial investigation found that there were no notifications of food poisoning incidents or an unusual increase in acute gastroenteritis (AGE) cases received through the existing surveillance system. JKNP announced that control and prevention actions being implemented include active case detection, epidemiological investigations to identify sources of exposure and related risk factors as well as environmental risk assessments in the recreational areas involved.

items.title: KL police use viral clip to track down teen over bike stunts from May (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 17 — A viral video of a motorcyclist performing stunts on the streets of the capital has led to an arrest, after the city’s Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department (JSPT) tracked down the offender. The investigation began after JSPT flagged a 1 minute 23 second clip uploaded to Facebook by account "Kyle Martopo." Police have since confirmed that the incident took place on May 17, at approximately 3.10am along Jalan Tun Razak.

items.title: Malaysia acquires RM423.8m ANKA‑S drones, capable of 24‑hour flights at 30,000 feet
items.summary: LABUAN, June 17 — The operation of the Royal Malaysian Air Force’s (RMAF) ANKA-S Unmanned Aircraft System (UAS) marks a strategic leap in strengthening the nation’s defence and maritime surveillance capabilities, Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said. He said the asset is capable of providing operational coverage previously unavailable, particularly in strategic maritime areas including the South China Sea, giving Malaysia a critical information advantage in safeguarding national security and sovereignty. “The operation of the ANKA-S aircraft represents a strategic leap in terms of our country’s defence and maritime surveillance.

items.title: Here comes the boom: Malaysia inks deal for France’s Caesar mobile artillery and local tech transfer
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 17 — Malaysia has significantly upgraded its land-based firepower, securing 18 Caesar artillery systems in a deal announced yesterday at the Eurosatory 2026 international defence and security exhibition. Representatives from KNDS and local industrial partner Advanced Defense System (ADS Sdn Bhd) inked the deal on Tuesday. In 2018, KNDS and Malaysian authorities reached a similar milestone with the local assembly of 105LG lightweight towed artillery guns.

items.title: Police bust student courier with RM1.17m ganja haul at Bukit Kayu Hitam
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, June 17 — Police have seized 14.63 kilogrammes (kg) of ganja buds worth an estimated RM1.17 million from a car at the Bukit Kayu Hitam Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) Complex. Kedah deputy police chief DCP Baderulhisham Baharudin said the 24-year-old Malaysian driver of the car, who is also a student at a private higher education institution in Kuala Lumpur, was arrested during the operation on Monday. “The total value of the drug seizure is RM1.17 million.
